what_it_does: Renders the top toolbar region for the Company canvas app: status/presence, mode dial, run button with inline error retry, graph tools (wire/portal/delete/layers/fit/point), reload, guided-tour launchers, and settings gear. It reads global state and actions from useApp and keeps a local mobile tools-fold state.
observations: Uses a local useState toolsOpen to collapse secondary controls on mobile while keeping workspace/status/mode always visible.
Renders an inline run-error surface with retry and dismiss buttons positioned near the run control.
Run and retry onClick handlers are arrow-wrapped (() => doRun()) to avoid passing React's MouseEvent into doRun as force.
Mode select and a separate mode-desc span show the current mode and its description.
Many controls carry data-ui-ref attributes such as ui://toolbar/run for addressing/guiding.
Guide and teach-me buttons invoke startGuide, optionally with the 'request-a-change' topic.
Settings gear opens a consolidated settings surface via openSettings().
Repeated conditional rendering guard {now && ...} for status-dependent elements.
standouts: The arrow-wrapped doRun onClick is explicitly load-bearing to prevent a canvas-RUN latch caused by passing MouseEvent as force.
Inline run-error recovery UI is co-located with the run button and clears on the next successful run.
Toolbar buttons are also addressable corpus elements, including the guide/teach and settings launchers.
conventions: Functional React component using hooks and context.
Heavy destructuring of useApp for state and callbacks.
ClassName strings built with concatenation rather than a class-utility.
Inline style marginLeft used for error-button spacing.
Comments map code to design/user-story IDs (U1, U4, U11, U6, C1, C2, A3).
data-ui-ref naming follows a ui://toolbar/<control> address scheme.
concerns: Large implicit dependency surface through destructured useApp; prop/data contracts are not visible in this file.
ClassName concatenation is brittle and mixes static and dynamic strings.
Inline style objects for spacing bypass the stylesheet.
No TypeScript props type or return type for Toolbar.
notes: A context-heavy top-bar component where most behavior is delegated to useApp, with careful local handling of the run button and mobile fold.
questions: What is the exact signature and behavior of doRun's optional force parameter?
How is layerView index mapped to the 'all'/'origin'/'system' labels beyond the three shown?
What does startGuide do when called without a topic?
How are data-ui-ref addresses registered and resolved by the guiding system?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments identify this as the toolbar region and the rendered elements correspond to that purpose.
intention_signals: Provide a persistent, status-rich command bar that prevents run-button latch failures, exposes graph editing and view controls, and doubles as an entry point for guided tours and self-modification workflows.
novelty: The toolbar treats its own controls as addressable corpus elements and includes a 'teach me to self-modify' button that launches a guided tour about requesting changes from inside the interface.
contribution: A top-level toolbar that consolidates run controls, mode selection, graph editing actions, layer/view toggles, guided-tour entry points, and settings access, with explicit safeguards around the run button and inline error recovery.
summary_for_embedding: Toolbar.tsx is a React toolbar component that renders the Company canvas app's top command bar, including presence, mode selection, run controls with inline error retry, graph tools, guided-tour launchers, and settings access, backed by useApp context and a local mobile fold state.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a single persistent operator surface for running the system, editing the graph, switching modes, and launching in-app guidance and settings.

symbols.does: Renders the top toolbar region with status, mode controls, run actions, graph tools, tours, and settings.
symbols.description: Toolbar is a functional React component that consumes useApp context to display the current presence/mode, run the system with an inline error-retry surface, trigger graph edits (wire/portal/delete/layers/fit/point), reload the view, launch guided tours, and open settings. It also manages a local mobile tools-fold state to hide secondary controls on small screens.
